ai_summary: Validate molecular spectroscopic constants (vibrational wavenumber and anharmonicity) for specific isotopologues of diatomic molecules from NIST WebBook and Huber-Herzberg molecular constants tables.
general_notes: All values refer to the electronic ground state of the specified diatomic isotopologue. Vibrational wavenumber we (omega_e) is the harmonic vibrational frequency in cm^-1. Anharmonicity constant wexe (omega_e * x_e) is in cm^-1. Both are Dunham expansion coefficients. Authoritative source: NIST Chemistry WebBook / Huber & Herzberg 'Molecular Spectra and Molecular Structure IV' (1979) / Irikura JPCRD 2007. These are fixed, well-tabulated constants.

columns.name: Molecule
columns.notes: Diatomic isotopologue notation: mass-number + element symbol hyphenated pairs (e.g. H-35Cl). Unique identifier.

columns.name: Vibrational Wavenumber we (cm-1)
columns.notes: Harmonic vibrational wavenumber omega_e for the ground electronic state in cm^-1. Source: NIST WebBook / Huber-Herzberg. Report to 3 decimal places for hydrogen-containing species, 1 decimal for heavier diatomics.

columns.name: Anharmonicity constant wexe (cm-1)
columns.notes: Anharmonicity constant omega_e * x_e for the ground electronic state in cm^-1. Source: NIST WebBook / Huber-Herzberg. Report to 3 decimal places for hydrogen-containing species, 2 decimal for heavier diatomics.
